nodes.label: Balances pallet
nodes.description: Runtime component that owns the native asset balance implementation.

nodes.label: Native balances ledger
nodes.description: Native currency account balances and total issuance exposed through the Balances adapter.

nodes.label: ORML Tokens pallet
nodes.description: Runtime component that owns balances and issuance for non-native, non-ERC20 assets.

nodes.label: ORML multi-token ledger
nodes.description: Per-asset account balances and issuance maintained by ORML Tokens.

nodes.label: ERC20 currency adapter
nodes.description: Runtime MultiCurrency adapter that executes ERC20 supply, balance, and transfer calls.

nodes.label: ERC20 contract ledgers
nodes.description: External EVM contract storage queried through totalSupply and balanceOf and mutated through ERC20 calls.

nodes.label: Currencies ledger router
nodes.description: MultiCurrency facade that dispatches operations to native Balances, bound ERC20 contracts, or ORML Tokens.

nodes.label: Routed total issuance provider
nodes.description: GetByKey provider that resolves an asset's total issuance through the Currencies routing rules.

nodes.label: StableSwap pallet
nodes.description: StableSwap pool component whose assets and share tokens use the routed currency interface.

nodes.label: StableSwap Pools storage
nodes.description: Pool configuration and asset membership keyed by the pool share asset identifier.

nodes.label: StableSwap reserve balances
nodes.description: Actual routed currency balances held by each StableSwap pool account.

nodes.label: StableSwap routed share ledger
nodes.description: Routed account balances and actual total issuance for the pool-id share asset.

nodes.label: StableSwap virtual share issuance
nodes.description: Pallet-tracked share issuance used for pool math and changed only by StableSwap mint and burn helpers.

nodes.label: StableSwap share issuance synchronization
nodes.description: Debug builds require exact equality; production rejects actual routed issuance greater than pallet-tracked virtual issuance.

evidence.symbol: stableswap: virtual share issuance out of sync with total issuance for pool {pool_id:?}

nodes.label: StableSwap add liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional operation that transfers reserves, mints pool shares, and checks the post-state invariant.

nodes.label: StableSwap remove liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional operation that transfers reserves out, burns pool shares, and checks the post-state invariant.

nodes.label: StableSwap add-liquidity invariant
nodes.description: Release-mode checks require D and D divided by issued shares to be non-decreasing after an add, except the initial issuance case.

nodes.label: StableSwap remove-liquidity invariant
nodes.description: Release-mode checks require D to fall while D divided by issued shares does not fall after a partial withdrawal.

nodes.label: XYK pallet
nodes.description: Constant-product pool component with routed reserve and share ledgers plus local liquidity accounting.

nodes.label: XYK ShareToken mapping
nodes.description: Mapping from a pair account to the routed asset identifier used for pool shares.

nodes.label: XYK TotalLiquidity
nodes.description: Pallet-local share total used as the issuance input to XYK liquidity calculations.

nodes.label: XYK pair-account reserves
nodes.description: Actual routed balances of both pool assets held by the deterministic pair account.

nodes.label: XYK routed share ledger
nodes.description: Account balances and issuance of the share-token asset selected by ShareToken.

nodes.label: XYK add liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional reserve transfer and coupled update of share balances and TotalLiquidity.

nodes.label: XYK remove liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional proportional reserve withdrawal and coupled reduction of shares and TotalLiquidity.

nodes.label: XYK share ledger equals TotalLiquidity
nodes.description: Expected equality maintained by adjacent mint-or-burn and TotalLiquidity writes; the liquidity paths do not perform an independent issuance equality assertion.

nodes.label: XYK proportional reserve backing
nodes.description: Liquidity-out calculations derive withdrawals from pair-account reserves, burned shares, and TotalLiquidity rather than asserting a separate global backing equation.

nodes.label: Omnipool pallet
nodes.description: Shared-liquidity pool component with stored asset state, NFT positions, and protocol-account ledgers.

nodes.label: Omnipool Assets state
nodes.description: Per-asset stored hub reserve, total shares, protocol shares, cap, and tradability; the asset reserve is loaded from the protocol account ledger.

nodes.label: Omnipool Positions state
nodes.description: NFT-position keyed liquidity ownership records containing an asset identifier and LP share amount.

nodes.label: Omnipool total shares
nodes.description: Assets.shares total from which protocol-owned shares are separated to derive user-position ownership.

nodes.label: Omnipool protocol shares
nodes.description: Subset of an asset's total pool shares owned by the protocol rather than LP positions.

nodes.label: Omnipool asset reserve ledger
nodes.description: Actual routed asset balances held in the Omnipool protocol account and joined with Assets state at load time.

nodes.label: Omnipool hub asset ledger
nodes.description: Hub asset balance held by the protocol account and expected to equal the aggregate stored hub reserves.

nodes.label: Omnipool add liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional operation that creates a position, moves the asset reserve, updates hub liquidity and Assets, then checks the curve invariant.

nodes.label: Omnipool remove liquidity
nodes.description: Transactional operation that reduces or removes a position, transfers reserve value, updates hub liquidity and Assets, then checks the curve invariant.

nodes.label: Omnipool LP position share sum
nodes.description: Try-runtime assertion that summed Position.shares equals Assets.shares minus Assets.protocol_shares for each represented asset.

nodes.label: Omnipool hub reserve backing
nodes.description: Try-runtime assertion that the protocol account hub balance equals the sum of Assets.hub_reserve.

nodes.label: Omnipool liquidity curve invariant
nodes.description: Release-mode bounded-product check between old and new asset and hub reserves after liquidity changes.

nodes.label: Circuit breaker pallet
nodes.description: Runtime component configured as the asset deposit limiter for issuance-rate protection.

nodes.label: ORML token deposit hook
nodes.description: ORML Tokens post-deposit hook boundary at which issuance increases are classified and excess deposits may be locked.

nodes.label: Issuance increase fuse
nodes.description: Preflight can_mint guard and post-deposit handler using current routed issuance, configured limits, and lockdown state; post-deposit handling locks excess rather than undoing issuance.

evidence.symbol: pub fn can_mint(currency_id: T::AssetId, amount: T::Balance) -> bool

nodes.label: Asset lockdown state
nodes.description: Per-asset locked or unlocked issuance-period state used by the deposit fuse.

nodes.label: Asset registry XCM rate limit
nodes.description: Optional per-asset issuance increase limit read from asset registry metadata.

edges.semantics: Balances is the configured runtime implementation for the native asset ledger.
ORML Tokens owns non-native multi-asset balances and issuance.
The adapter exposes ERC20 contract storage through the runtime currency interface; the contracts remain the system of record.
The native currency id is dispatched through the BasicCurrencyAdapter backed by Balances.

evidence.symbol: type NativeCurrency = BasicCurrencyAdapter<Runtime, Balances, Amount, BlockNumber>;

edges.semantics: Asset ids bound to an ERC20 address are dispatched to the Erc20Currency adapter.
Non-native asset ids without an ERC20 binding are dispatched to ORML Tokens.
The issuance provider delegates to Currencies.total_issuance and therefore inherits its three-way ledger routing.
StableSwap reserve reads and transfers use Currencies, so each reserve resolves to Balances, ORML Tokens, or an ERC20 contract according to its asset id.
StableSwap share issuance and balances use the configured Currencies implementation.
XYK reserve reads and transfers use Currencies, preserving native, ORML, and ERC20 routing.
XYK share mint and burn operations use the configured Currencies implementation.
Omnipool asset reserve balances use Currencies and therefore may reside in native, ORML, or ERC20 ledgers.
Omnipool hub asset accounting uses the configured Currencies implementation.
StableSwap owns pool configuration and asset membership storage.
StableSwap controls reserve balances held in its pool accounts through routed transfers.
The pool id is also the routed share asset minted and burned by StableSwap.
StableSwap owns the virtual issuance used for all share calculations.
StableSwap checks its virtual issuance against the routed ledger after guarded state transitions.
StableSwap implements the add-liquidity state transition.
StableSwap implements the remove-liquidity state transition.
Share calculation begins from actual pool-account balances.
Share calculation and the invariant use pallet-tracked virtual share issuance.
The calculated pool shares are deposited to the provider's routed share balance.
The virtual issuance is increased before the routed share deposit in the same transaction.
Provided assets are transferred into the pool account before the final invariant check.
A failing D or per-share D check returns InvariantError and rolls back the transactional operation.
The invariant recomputes D from post-transfer pool-account reserves.
The invariant uses current virtual issuance as the denominator of post-add D per share.
The post-add invariant first verifies virtual issuance against routed total issuance.
Withdrawal amounts are calculated from actual pool-account reserves.
The operation reads current virtual share issuance to calculate proportional reserve output.
The withdrawn pool share amount is burned from the provider's routed balance.
The virtual issuance is decreased before the routed share withdrawal in the same transaction.
Calculated amounts leave the pool account before the post-state check.
A failing D or per-share D check returns InvariantError and rolls back the transactional withdrawal.
The invariant recomputes D from post-transfer pool-account reserves.
The invariant uses current virtual issuance as the denominator of post-withdrawal D per share.
The post-withdrawal invariant first verifies virtual issuance against routed total issuance.
Mint and burn helpers couple virtual issuance updates with routed ledger mutations; the synchronization invariant detects excess routed issuance.
The synchronization check reads pallet-tracked virtual issuance.
The synchronization check reads routed total issuance and rejects values above virtual issuance.
XYK owns the pair-account to share-asset mapping.
XYK owns its local total-liquidity accounting value.
XYK controls routed reserve balances at the pair account.
XYK mints and burns the share asset selected by ShareToken.
The mapping selects which routed asset id represents shares for a pair account.
The add calculation reads both actual pair-account reserves.
TotalLiquidity is used as share issuance in the add-liquidity calculation.
Both supplied assets are transferred to the pair account transactionally.
Calculated shares are deposited into the routed share ledger.
The local total is increased immediately after the routed share mint in the same transaction.
The withdrawal calculation reads actual pair-account reserves.
TotalLiquidity supplies the share denominator for proportional output.
Calculated reserve amounts are transferred from the pair account.
Removed shares are withdrawn from the routed share ledger.
The local total is reduced immediately after the routed share burn in the same transaction.
TotalLiquidity is expected to equal routed share issuance, but production liquidity paths maintain this through coupled writes rather than an independent equality check.
A share's withdrawal value is calculated proportionally from actual reserves and TotalLiquidity; no separate global backing assertion is made here.
The equality is a semantic obligation created by XYK's duplicate share accounting.
XYK liquidity calculations define proportional reserve backing for its shares.
Omnipool owns per-asset aggregate state.
Omnipool owns NFT-position keyed LP state.
Assets stores the aggregate shares field for each pool asset.
Assets separately stores the protocol-owned subset of aggregate shares.
Loading a usable reserve state joins stored Assets fields with the actual protocol-account asset balance.
For each asset, summed Position.shares must equal Assets.shares minus Assets.protocol_shares.
The sum of Assets.hub_reserve must equal the protocol account's hub asset balance.
The supplied asset amount is transferred to the protocol account.
A new LP position containing the calculated shares is inserted.
The updated aggregate asset state is persisted after reserve and hub updates.
Hub asset liquidity is updated from the calculated delta in the same transaction.
The completed transition calls the release-mode curve check and rolls back on error.
Calculated asset value is transferred out of the protocol account.
The LP position is reduced or removed according to the withdrawn shares.
The updated aggregate asset state is persisted after withdrawal calculations.
Hub asset liquidity is adjusted from the calculated removal delta.
The completed transition calls the release-mode curve check and rolls back on error.
The bounded-product check uses old and new actual asset reserve values.
The bounded-product check uses old and new hub reserve state.
Try-runtime aggregates shares from every stored LP position.
Try-runtime compares the aggregate position total with the per-asset total shares field.
Protocol-owned shares are subtracted before comparison with user LP positions.
Try-runtime sums every stored Assets.hub_reserve before comparing it with the ledger balance.
Try-runtime reads the protocol account hub asset balance for comparison.
The circuit breaker provides the issuance fuse used by the token deposit hook.
The circuit breaker stores each asset's issuance period or lockdown state.
ORML Tokens invokes the fuse after a deposit through CurrencyHooks.PostDeposit.
The fuse obtains current issuance through AssetTotalIssuance, so native, ERC20, and ORML routing semantics apply.
The per-asset deposit limit is supplied from Asset Registry xcm_rate_limit metadata.
Classification depends on whether the asset is unlocked, actively locked, or has an expired period.
Post-deposit handling resets the issuance window or records lockdown through circuit-breaker state transitions.
When post-deposit growth exceeds the limit, excess balance is reserved and the asset enters lockdown; issuance itself is not rolled back by this hook.
can_mint can reject a proposed mint before execution when its caller uses the preflight API; the configured ORML hook is separately post-deposit.

evidence.symbol: pub fn can_mint(currency_id: T::AssetId, amount: T::Balance) -> bool
